slides.title: Multiple Conditions 🚥
slides.content:
Use the `else if` statement to specify a new condition if the first condition is `false`.

You can chain as many `else if` statements as you want!

slides.title: Example

quizData.question: What will be printed in the example above?
quizData.explanation: Since 85 is not >= 90, the first condition fails. The compiler then checks the `else if` condition (85 >= 80), which is true, so it prints 'B' and ignores the rest.

slides.title: Top to Bottom ⬇️
slides.content:
The compiler checks `if` and `else if` statements sequentially from top to bottom.

As soon as it finds a `true` condition, it executes that block and **ignores** all the following `else if` or `else` blocks in the chain.

quizData.question: If multiple conditions in an if/else if chain are true, how many blocks of code will be executed?

options.text: All blocks where the condition is true
Only the first one that is true
Only the last one that is true

quizData.explanation: The block corresponding to the 'first' true condition from top to bottom is executed. Then the rest of the chain is skipped entirely.
